title: cleomella brevipes
intro: Cleomella brevipes, also known as Mexican poppy or California poppy, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Cleomella brevipes include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for herbal remedies, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
